tier: A2-3.1 production raw-device mutation path certified on physical hardware
host: Local WSL2 archlinux (qemu-system-x86_64 -enable-kvm, macOS Sequoia recovery, apfs_kext) with the physical disk passed through (wsl --mount --bare -> /dev/sdf -> qemu -drive file=/dev/sdf)
device: Seagate Expansion Desk, serial NAAA3QVK, 8 TB (8001563221504 bytes / 7.28 TiB), USB, disposable
container: 256 GiB (274877906944 bytes) Apple_APFS GPT partition (Disk 2 Partition 2 -> /dev/sdf2 -> macOS disk0s2 -> synthesized container disk1), multi-CIB tier, volume SAKPROD
production_path: The actual shipping production code: PartitionApfsWriter::commitRawFileWrite / commitRawFileDelete / commitRawFileRename (what FileManagementFileSystemBridge calls), driven through the sak_apfs_writer_cli commit-raw-file-write / commit-raw-file-delete / commit-raw-file-rename commands on \\.\Harddisk2Partition2 with --confirm-target --allow-raw-target.
operations: commit-raw-file-write doc.txt replace 'prod-raw-write-v2-noticeably-longer-content' (delete xid 3->4, insert xid 4->5)
linux_apfsck: apfsck -w clean on /dev/sdf2 after all mutations - only the pre-existing benign 'Data stream xfield: wrong flags.' apfsck-vs-Apple divergence

apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_mount_read: the macOS kernel auto-mounted the real-drive partition read-write at /Volumes/SAKPROD; ls showed the final state (renamed.txt present, note.txt deleted, doc.txt renamed away) and cat returned 'prod-raw-write-v2-noticeably-longer-content' (the replace payload, intact through write->replace->rename)
apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_continuation: writer emitted xid 8; the kernel mounted read-write and CONTINUED the checkpoint to transaction ID 10 (two kernel commits past the writer)
apple_fsck_apfs.command: fsck_apfs -n /dev/disk1 (the synthesized container, unmounted after the kernel auto-mounted it read-write)
apple_fsck_apfs.container_verdict: The container /dev/disk1 appears to be OK.
apple_fsck_apfs.volume_verdict: The volume /dev/rdisk1s1 (UUID 9FA952B3-78DA-46AA-B86B-7FB4FB3EE0DE) appears to be OK.
apple_fsck_apfs.volume_provenance: The volume SAKPROD was formatted by S.A.K. Utility APFS writer and last modified by apfs_kext.
apple_fsck_apfs.evidence: apfs-prod-raw-physical-fsck-clean.png (fsck verdict), apfs-prod-raw-physical-kernel-read.png (kernel ls + disk layout), apfs-prod-raw-physical-cat-unmount.png (cat renamed.txt + unmount)

writer_dead_zone_finding: The full 7.28 TiB whole-disk geometry is currently unsupported (format-raw fail-closes): 474 inline cib-address entries overflow the spaceman block, but cib_count < 507 so the CAB tier does not engage. The 3-7.8 TiB whole-disk range is a writer dead zone; the CAB-tier threshold should engage on inline-array overflow rather than the fixed cibs_per_cab (507). This cert used a supported-size partition; the dead-zone fix is a separate follow-on.
